literal_meaning: and what, bhikkhus, are the five clung-to heaps
preferred_translation: and what, bhikkhus, are the five clung-to heaps
alternative_translations: and what, bhikkhus, are the five heaps taken personally
discouraged_translations: and what are the five heaps of attachment
definition: Canonical heading for the five clung-to heaps and their constituent enumeration.
notes: This phrase governs the appropriated version of the family and should stay aligned with the fixed whole-expression policy for pañcupādānakkhandhā. The shorter clung-to wording remains the default. Use the longer heaps-taken-personally alternate only when the local prose needs the appropriative force spelled out more explicitly.

context_rules.context: In direct doctrinal translation of the standard heading
context_rules.rendering: and what, bhikkhus, are the five clung-to heaps

context_rules.context: When explanatory prose needs the appropriative force highlighted
context_rules.rendering: and what, bhikkhus, are the five heaps taken personally
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate for explicitness.

example_phrases.translation: and what, bhikkhus, are the five clung-to heaps?
example_phrases.notes: Canonical heading for the appropriated set.
